name: Make (Integromat) API
what_it_does: Make (formerly Integromat) REST API for managing scenarios (workflows), executions, and teams — enabling programmatic control of visual workflow automation across 1000+ integrated apps.
use_cases: Triggering Make scenarios via webhooks from custom applications
Monitoring execution logs and error rates across automations
Creating and deploying scenarios via API for template-driven automation
Building complex multi-step data transformation pipelines visually
not_for: Very simple single-step integrations (Zapier may be easier)
High-code custom automation requiring full programming control (use n8n)
Teams needing fully self-hosted automation without vendor
best_when: You need complex multi-step automation with iterators, aggregators, and data transformations in a visual environment with more power than Zapier.
avoid_when: You need real-time sub-second execution, code-level control, or fully self-hosted automation.

auth.notes: API tokens generated in Make account settings. Passed as Authorization Bearer header. Token provides access to all scenarios in the account. Team/organization tokens available for shared access.

pricing.paid_tiers: Core: $10.59/month (10k ops); Pro: $18.82/month (10k ops + advanced features); Teams: $34.12/month
pricing.notes: Operations-based pricing (each module execution = 1 operation). Very cost-effective compared to Zapier for complex scenarios. Enterprise pricing available.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on plan; webhook triggers limited by plan tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API errors include status codes and messages. Scenario execution errors logged with detail in Make UI but less accessible via API.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Triggering the same webhook multiple times creates multiple executions.
known_agent_gotchas: Scenarios execute asynchronously — webhook trigger returns immediately, results must be fetched via execution logs
Operation counting differs from Zapier tasks — loops multiply operations significantly
Webhook custom responses require specific scenario design — not automatic
Data mapping and transformation uses Make's own syntax (not JavaScript/Python) in the visual editor
Free tier 1000 operations/month can be consumed quickly by looping scenarios
Scenario blueprints (JSON export/import) enable programmatic scenario deployment but format is undocumented
